item.title: Hicks — Sixties Rock
item.description: Author: Michael Hicks Title: Sixties Rock: Garage, Psychedelic, and Other Satisfactions Year: 1999 Type: Academic book (University of Illinois Press) The book’s lasting tool is a three-part answer to a question most writing about psychedelia never asks precisely: what does the music actually do? Hic...

item.title: Chapman — Psychedelia and Other Colours
item.description: Author: Rob Chapman Title: Psychedelia and Other Colours Year: 2015 Type: Book (Faber & Faber) The book’s founding move is to split psychedelia in two.

item.title: DeRogatis — Turn On Your Mind
item.description: Author: Jim DeRogatis Title: Turn On Your Mind: Four Decades of Great Psychedelic Rock Year: 2003 Type: Book (Hal Leonard) The definition does the book’s heaviest lifting.

item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Era1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976)GenresFolk rockContemporary folkSinger-songwriterBlues rockCountry rockSceneGreenwich Village folk sceneListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an acoustic guitar a...

item.title: Carole King
item.description: Era1958–present (primary period: 1960–1971)GenresPop soulBrill BuildingSinger-songwriterGirl group (as songwriter)Folk popSceneLaurel Canyon (1968–1975)ListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The opening chord of “I Feel the Earth Move” is a left-hand piano pattern that could be R&B — percussive, rhythmic...

item.title: Dionne Warwick
item.description: Era1962–present (primary period: 1962–1971)GenresPop soulBrill BuildingStandardsListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Listen to the way Dionne Warwick navigates the melody of “Walk on By” — the intervals are wide, and the phrasing syncopates against Burt Bacharach’s rhythm track in ways that make the emotio...

item.title: Dusty Springfield
item.description: Era1958–1999 (primary period: 1963–1969)GenresPop soulBlue-eyed soulSoulSceneLondon; Memphis; New YorkListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Mary Isobel Catherine Bernadette O’Brien, from West Hampstead, London, became Dusty Springfield and sang Black American music with a devotion and a vocal intelligence t...

item.title: Elvis Presley
item.description: Era1954–1977GenresRock & rollRockabillyRhythm & bluesPopCountryGospelSceneMemphis (Sun Records, RCA)ListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The voice was the instrument.

item.title: Laura Nyro
item.description: Era1966–1997 (primary period: 1967–1971)GenresPop soulSinger-songwriterProgressive soulBrill BuildingSceneNew York CityListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The piano on “Timer” (1968) shifts from a ballad’s tenderness to a percussive, almost violent intensity in the space of a breath — Laura Nyro’s left ha...
